summary: Cathy O'Neil announces the launch of the Lede Program at Columbia Journalism School, a data journalism certificate program she's helping to lead. The post shares practical details about upcoming information sessions and the four summer courses being offered. She expresses particular enthusiasm for the final course on platforms, which will examine how biases in data systems affect our understanding of truth. The post also serves as a call for instructors to teach the intensive seven-week summer courses.
key_points: The Lede Program is a new data journalism initiative at Columbia designed to teach journalists data practices
The curriculum covers four areas: basic computing, data and databases, algorithms, and platforms
The platform course will specifically investigate biases inherent in data systems and how they shape perceptions of objective truth
The program is actively recruiting instructors for intensive seven-week summer sessions
money_quotes: I really cherish that addiction like a child. Don't tell my other kids.
We will investigate biases inherent in data, systems, and platforms and how they affect our understanding of objective truth.
key_insight: Training journalists to understand data systems must include examining the biases inherent in those systems and how they shape what we consider objective truth.
